entry.title: Re: CAP-consuming on-premise OData API : read ECONNRESET...
entry.content:
I also adding this `ui5.yaml` proxy configuration fixed the **ECONNRESET** error in my UI5 app 

**What was happening before**

My app was calling the backend directly from the browser, for example: * https://approuter-test.../api-je * http://127.0.0.1:3050 This caused classic connection issues: * CORS restrictions * HTTPS → HTTP mixed content blocking * SSL certificate validation problems * BAS / browser security policies The browser tried to connect directly and the connection was being closed, resulting in `ECONNRESET`. ---

**What changed with ui5-middleware-simpleproxy**

After adding the proxy middleware, the flow became: Browser → UI5 Dev Server → Proxy → Real Backend Instead of calling the backend directly, the UI now calls local paths like: ``` /api /api-je ``` The UI5 dev server forwards the request to the real endpoint behind the scenes. This solves multiple problems at once: * The browser sees requests as same-origin → no CORS issues * No mixed-content errors (HTTPS frontend can reach HTTP backend) * SSL validation is handled by the middleware instead of the browser * Network restrictions from BAS or CF are avoided ---

**Key parts that made it work** *

`mountPath` creates a local endpoint that the UI calls * `beforeMiddleware: serveResources` ensures requests are intercepted early * `strictSSL: false` allows local HTTP or self-signed certs without connection resets Example: ``` fetch("/api") // works fetch("http://127.0.0.1:3050") // caused ECONNRESET before ``` ---

**Short explanation** The ECONNRESET disappeared because the browser stopped calling the backend directly. The UI5 dev server proxy now handles the connection, avoiding CORS, SSL, and mixed-content issues.

entry.title: Re: Programmatically retrieving lineage/source object inf...
entry.content: Hi @CLTGravesen, Thank you very much for taking the time to write such a detailed explanation, it really helped me move in the right direction. 🙂 Based on your suggestion about using the CLI READ OBJECT output, I extracted the CSN file for my Models and was actually able to trace a field back to its originating Datasphere view and further down to the source table by programmatically parsing the dependency tree in the CSN. It definitely required some effort to interpret the structure, but your hint that the dependency information exists there was the key insight I was looking for. Really appreciate you sharing your experience and pointing me toward the CLI approach. Thanks again for your help! Best, Shubham

entry.title: Re: Salesforce Adapter download option not found
entry.content:
Adapter Installation on Cloud Foundry can be done following below steps.

Same is available in the documentation - https://api.sap.com/package/SalesforceAdapter/documents - Salesforce Adapter and Plug-in Installation Guide 

1. Navigate to the Discover tab.

2. Using the search box, search for “Salesforce Adapter for SAP Integration Suite”.

3. Select the “Salesforce Adapter for SAP Integration Suite” package.

4. Navigate to the Copy option. Click Copy to replicate the package from the Discover workspace to the Design workspace.

5. Navigate to the Design workspace and select the Salesforce Adapter for SAP Integration Suite package.

6. Select Artifacts tab and choose the Deploy action to deploy the Salesforce Adapter.

entry.title: Re: Integrating low code solutions with Microsoft using SAP Integration Suite has never been easier!
entry.content:
Hello Martin,

Again thanks for your blog. It assumes that we are receiving from EntraId a JWT token but it the sender receives an SAML assertion from EntraId instead of the JWT token how will be the process of Principal Propagation? easier right? because the token exchange wouldnt be necessary?

Thanks

Jorge

entry.title: Maintaining of Usage attribute
entry.content: Is there a different way on maintaining the Usage Attribute in the Packaging Data based on the Company Role. For Example for producers selling e.g. produced packaging to another company who is using it to package their products. Would the input be commercial since they aren't selling it to the end-consumer or would it be houshould because it will eventually end up with the end-consumer?

entry.title: Re: How to properly use BAdI SD_SLS_DATA_TRANSFER_HEAD?
entry.content:
Hello @bigflatfoot

First, you need to configure copy routine. 

--> go to app Manage Your Solution --> 103089

--> Create routine 

--> Assign enhancement id

once that is done, you can implement this badi in Custom logic and set up filter as Enchancement_ID = QUOTE. 

best regards, 

Freskita

entry.title: Re: Integrating low code solutions with Microsoft using SAP Integration Suite has never been easier!
entry.content:
Hey @JorgeCalderon,

the flow requires a SAML assertion to be passed to the SAP OAuth server for exchange. This article describes how to get a trusted SAML assertion issued by Entra ID for any known client app that wants to talk to SAP. If you generate your own SAML assertion you obtain the responsibility of an IDP like Entra yourself with all the risks that come with it.

The flow can not be shortened without compromising security.

Let the community know what you decided and why in the end 🙂

entry.title: Re: An End-to-End Hands-On Tutorial for Clean Core RAP Development
entry.content:
Hello Iulia, thanks for your fantastic work!

I have a problem in step 4 (create behavior definition), i retrieve error in line:

"create ( authorization : global );"

with message: "none" was expected, not "global". 

Any ideas about how solve this?

 

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Record quantity indicator in center cost
entry.content:
Hi @Chris1973 after reviewing the documentation, I still don't see any changes in the quantity field for accounts 6, which are expense accounts... I ran the test with the check active for both the cost center and the account.

The test I performed is as follows: I entered goods through the MIGO application for a stock order, and I see the quantity field reflected in account 3, which is stock with operation type BSX, but I don't see it in the expense and stock variation accounts with operation types EIN and EKG, which is where I want to see it. (The quantity record field is activated in both the expense account and the inventory variation account and and in both cost centers). I am attaching a screenshot of my view of the item with the quantity field.

Best regards.

entry.content:
Good afternoon @Anabela_Lucio 
Hope you are well.

Are there any updates on this subject?

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Bin sorting in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @ST24,

In Public Cloud WM you can mass-upload / maintain bin sorting via the Migration Cockpit migration object Warehouse storage bin sorting. SAP explicitly states that updating existing bin sorting data is supported (file upload) and also mentions the practical limitation that you typically need a new migration project for repeated uploads of the same key combination

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/c5adf62e525b488290a6df8f4dd7da8a.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/61fa9952db084ac2a785160cc5c5191e.html

IMPORTANT: Those WM/EWM migration objects might be missing in Migration Cockpit unless the right scope is active. SAP Note lists Warehouse storage bin sorting among the WM objects and explains the symptom

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2884164

So, yes upload is possible in Public Cloud just not via the old classic EWM but via Migration Cockpit

How do you get sorting

SAP confirms the key behavior:

  • In Public Cloud WM a default bin sorting record is generated for each newly created storage bin
  • Then the migration object Warehouse storage bin sorting is available to update that generated default sorting data

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/c5adf62e525b488290a6df8f4dd7da8a.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/61fa9952db084ac2a785160cc5c5191e.html

Use the Sort Storage Bins app (/SCWM/SBST) to (re)generate the bin sorting for the relevant warehouse + activity + activity area

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('%252FSCWM%252FSBST')/S36

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/b7f2906dc5b94711aa7f139c4105c17b.html

SAP even provides in Note how to sequence for this topic:

  1. Go to customizing: Assign Storage Bins to Activity Areas
  2. Got to customizing: Define Sort Sequence for Activity Area
  3. Run Sort Storage Bins app (/SCWM/SBST)
  4. Verify in Display Storage Bin app

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3590140

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3676710

Additional information:

For mass bin creation you can use:

  • Create Multiple Storage Bins and SAP WM overview also lists Mass Create / Mass Edit Storage Bins type apps as available capabilities in this area

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/9980c8e010a3459a9263e270c01cad7e.html

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('%252FSCWM%252FLS11')/S36

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('%25252FSCWM%25252FLS10')/S36

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification for Partners: An Overview of the Latest Changes - Including Q&A

thanks for your question. I don’t have a dedicated, partner‑specific blog post at hand right now, but the following resources explain the overall direction SAP Certification is taking towards 2026:

Christina

Pavan K

entry.title: SAP HANA System Replication Takeover and Client Connectivity
entry.content:
Documented Scope and Architectural Responsibilities


Introduction
This article summarizes publicly available SAP documentation and reflects the documented behavior of SAP HANA System Replication at the time of writing.

SAP HANA System Replication is widely used in productive landscapes to improve availability and to enable takeover to a secondary system.
In operational situations, however, a recurring observation can occur:

After a takeover, the secondary system assumes the primary database role, while application connectivity depends on the surrounding client access path.

This article does not provide configuration or implementation guidance.
Instead, it consolidates SAP documentation to clarify the documented scope of System Replication and the responsibilities described in related high-availability integration documentation.

The purpose is to align operational expectations with documented behavior.


Documented Purpose of System Replication
According to SAP documentation, SAP HANA System Replication continuously transfers database changes from a primary system to a secondary system and supports a role change (takeover).

SAP documentation describes that System Replication:

  • replicates database persistence (data and log changes)
  • enables takeover to a secondary system
  • preserves transactional consistency

Sources
SAP HANA System Replication Guide
https://help.sap.com/doc/c81e9406d08046c0a118c8bef71f6bdc/2.0.07/en-US/SAP_HANA_System_Replication_Guide_en.pdf
SAP Help Portal – System Replication Overview
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/676844172c2442f0bf6c8b080db05ae7.html

After takeover, the secondary system becomes the new primary database system from the database perspective.
The documentation therefore describes System Replication primarily in terms of database availability and data consistency.


Documented Behavior During Takeover
During a takeover, SAP HANA changes the database role of the participating systems and starts the required database services on the new primary host.

From the database perspective described in the System Replication documentation, the takeover is considered completed once the primary role is active and database services are operational on the new primary host according to the System Replication documentation.

The System Replication Guide documents the database role change and service startup behavior.
The documentation describes database state and service availability, while network routing and client access handling are described separately in high-availability integration documentation.


Actions After Takeover in HA Integration Documentation
SAP documentation describes integration mechanisms that allow external components to react to a role change.

SAP HANA provides a HA/DR provider hook that enables external high-availability components to execute actions after a takeover.

Documented examples include service endpoint switching or cluster-related actions.

Sources
Implementing SAP HANA HA/DR Providers
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/1200ab8ef0c54c54be2d0e7f5327f7ed.html
Installing and Configuring HA/DR Provider Hook
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/2962efcfdd6740689d5705bdabe9a2d5.html

The HA/DR integration documentation describes coordination with external high-availability mechanisms following a database role change.


Database Role and Client Connectivity in Documented Architecture
System Replication changes the database role.
Client connectivity depends on the access path used by the application.

Typical productive environments use infrastructure components such as:

  • virtual IP addresses
  • cluster frameworks
  • load balancers
  • DNS-based host names

SAP System Replication documentation describes the database role and persistence synchronization, while HA integration documentation describes coordination with external availability mechanisms.
These documents therefore address different parts of the overall availability architecture.


Interpreting Connection Failures After Takeover
In operational situations, a takeover may be completed from the database perspective while application connections still fail if the client access path still targets the former primary host or has not yet been updated by the surrounding infrastructure.

From a documentation perspective, this reflects different layers of the availability architecture rather than a single mechanism.

SAP documentation separates:

  • database role change (System Replication)
  • external availability coordination (HA integration)

Reference
SAP Note 1999880 - FAQ: SAP HANA System Replication


Relationship to High Availability Architecture
SAP HANA high availability consists of multiple documented components, including:

  • System Replication
  • cluster or HA integration
  • infrastructure routing mechanisms

System Replication addresses database availability.
Other components coordinate access paths and service endpoints in the surrounding infrastructure.


Conclusion
SAP HANA System Replication provides database availability and transactional consistency after a takeover as described in the official documentation.

Client connectivity depends on surrounding high-availability and infrastructure mechanisms that are documented separately from System Replication.

Understanding the documented scope of these components helps distinguish database state from client access routing and supports clearer troubleshooting during incidents.

For implementation or configuration guidance, always follow the official SAP documentation and SAP recommendations.


Disclaimer
This article summarizes publicly available SAP documentation and does not replace official SAP configuration, sizing, or implementation guidance. This article describes documented architectural behavior and is not an SAP support statement.

entry.title: IBOP – Strategic Power of Unrestricted Checks, Confirmation Protection, Rounding and More!
entry.content:
If you haven't already, have a look at the first blog to understand the foundation of Interactive Backorder Processing (IBOP) before diving into the massive leap forward we've taken with the 2025 release series.

Scaling Interactive Backorder Processing in the 2025 Series

When the Manage Confirmations for Backorders Fiori app first launched with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS03, it revolutionized the "Order Fulfillment Specialist" role. It moved us away from static, scheduled automation toward a dynamic, real-time workspace where you could manually redistribute confirmations using strategies like Improve, Redistribute, or Lose.

While the 2023 release (FPS03) established the foundation for manual control + mass recheck, the 2025 release series shifts from simple adjustment to strategic command and informed decision making.

2025 FPS0: The "Superpower" of Unrestricted Checks

The 2025 FPS0 release introduces a feature that completely redefines the boundaries of aATP: Unrestricted Checks (Override Capabilities).

Breaking the Constraints

In standard ATP checks, the system acts as a strict gatekeeper, ensuring you never promise more than what is logically available. However, critical business moments — like securing a massive order for a VIP customer — demand the power to override those rules. Authorized users (via the CA_ATP_IAB authorization object) can now selectively bypass the following gatekeepers:

  • Sales Product Allocation (Sales PAL )
  • Product Availability Check (PAC) and Supply Protection (SUP)
  • Capacity Product Allocation (Capacity PAL)

Mass Recheck:

 

Manual Change:

How powerful is this?

Imagine a scenario where you have only 100 units of unrestricted stock, but a VIP customer needs 500 units. A normal check would fail for the additional 400 units. In Unrestricted Mode, you can bypass PAC and PAL constraints to grant a full confirmation of 500 units, even if it results in over-consumption (negative stock). This gives you the ultimate authority to prioritize critical business commitments over standard system limits.

The availability constraint status is depicted below: 

Transparency and Governance

With great power comes the need for traceability. It is crucial to highlight that once you publish these changes, any requirements checked as unrestricted against basic methods are logged in the Monitor BOP Application. This ensures that every strategic "override" is fully visible and auditable within the system's history.

Note: Logging in BOP Monitor is available only from 2025FPS1 release onwards.

2025 FPS0: Securing Your Decisions: Fixed Date and Quantity

To prevent your manual heroics from being overturned by the next automated Backorder Processing (BOP) run, 2025 FPS0 introduces the Set Fixed Date and Quantity option. Once "fixed," the system applies a "Skip" strategy during subsequent BOP runs, ensuring your manual commitments remain untouched.

To set the flag on an ATP-checked requirement:

Similarly, to reset:

2025 FPS1: Precision and Harmony

Moving into 2025 FPS1, you can experience sophisticated behavior with System Delivery Proposals and full support for Rounding Profiles, ensuring your manual edits are both free-handed (i.e., the system does not revert the confirmation to its previous state in case the request cannot be met) and precisely rounded to the nearest quantity.

  • System Delivery Proposals: Speed is essential in high-volume environments. By enabling the "Accept system delivery proposal?" feature in your App Settings, the system will automatically propose confirmations based on the real-time stock situation during manual updates, drastically reducing the manual data entry and clicks needed to finalize your worklist.

  • Respecting Rounding Profiles: For businesses with strict packaging units, the system now fully respects Rounding Profiles. Any manual confirmation change is automatically adjusted to align with your maintained rounding configurations, ensuring technical accuracy.

Looking ahead, the future of Fulfillment is Interactive!

The evolution from 2023 FPS03 to the 2025 FPS1 release showcases a clear trajectory: providing users with immediate, interactive control without sacrificing the underlying governance of real-time ATP. Whether you are overriding restrictions to save a VIP relationship or using correlation groups to synchronize a complex shipment, IBOP ensures that your supply chain remains flexible, transparent, and most importantly, responsive to the speed of business.

For more information, please refer to:

  • 2025FPS0
  • 2025FPS1

Watch this space for more series of exciting features in the upcoming release. Stay tuned!

Kind regards,
Shishira Shastri H

entry.title: SAP Community CPH Event Plan - 2026
entry.content:
SAP Community Copenhagen – Event Plan for 2026

We are excited to share our SAP Community plan for 2026!

This year we are aiming at organizing at least four events – one per quarter – combining professional inspiration with cozy community social time.

In Q1, before Easter, we will do a social meet-up. This will be a great opportunity to reconnect, meet new members, and strengthen our community. The date will be the 26th of March and the event for sign up will be shared shortly.

In Q2, at the end of May after the SAPPHIRE conference in Madrid (19-21 May), we will host an event where we would like to invite 1–2 speakers from Denmark or abroad to share key announcements and highlights from SAPPHIRE. We are aiming at the 28th of May for this event.

After the summer holidays, in Q3, we will meet again for a social event while the weather is still good – a perfect setting for socializing and sharing our holiday pictures.

Finally, after SAP TechEd in the fourth quarter, we are planning a half-day or full-day event with inspirational talks from speakers from Denmark and abroad, focusing on the latest trends and insights. 

The dates for the second half of 2026 are still TBD. 

On behalf of the SAP Community CPH organizers, we are really looking forward to an inspiring year together and to growing our SAP Community even stronger in 2026!

Juan, Prakash, Pallavi, Christian, Asier

entry.title: Re: SAP Community CPH Event Plan - 2026
entry.content: Looking forward

entry.title: Re: Transport Request Error Object TVDIR,TDDAT cannot be locked
entry.content: hi @De81 Devyani Patil , first and foremost, that code looks to me a warning and not an error. And in regard to the message, it comes to my mind that you may have hit the moment someone else was releasing a transport OR a programmer forced in the transport request some T*DIR entries. Lastly, if the transport got released you may be good BUT you should check what entries are getting exported in the transport request before importing it in target systems.

entry.title: Re: Custom Cube for Margin Analysis - Calculated Key Figure shows no data
entry.content:
Hello Koushik, thank you for your reply. The calculated measure is already blank in ADT preview.

Please have a look at the screenshot. The Preview shows single rows. On single row level the calculation is of course impossible. It needs to aggregate the rows first so that RevenueGlobalCurr and COGSGlobalCurr are summarized.

But ADT is not doing it.

 

I am using DefaultAggregation SUM in the Restricted Measure Cube

//// Restricted Measures ////
  
    @EndUserText.label: 'Revenue'
    @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'GlobalCurrency'
    @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
    case when SemanticTag = 'ZGS_C_PRIC'
    and Ledger = '0L'
    then curr_to_decfloat_amount ( AmountInGlobalCurrency )
    else null
    end as RevenueGlobalCurr,
    
  
    @EndUserText.label: 'COGS'
    @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'GlobalCurrency'
    @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
    case when SemanticTag = 'ZGS_L_PRIC'
    and Ledger = '0L'
    then curr_to_decfloat_amount ( AmountInGlobalCurrency )
    else null
    end as COGSGlobalCurr,

And also using it in the Calculcated Measure Cube

 

////  Key Figures with Semantic Tag

  @EndUserText.label: 'Revenue'
  @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'GlobalCurrency'
    RevenueGlobalCurr,
    
  @EndUserText.label: 'COGS'
  @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'GlobalCurrency'
    COGSGlobalCurr,

It looks like the aggregation is not working as intended? What can be done to fix it?

I also removed all dimensions that are not needed to allow aggregation but this also did not fix the issue.

Thank you

David

entry.title: Re: Reading GOS(Generic Object Services) in MM03

entry.title: Re: Service Entry Sheet Performance period is not defaulted
entry.content: Thanks David for your reply, We have a valid performance start and end date in PO. Still not showing by default in the SES on reference to PO. I had raised a SAP case in this regard.

entry.title: Re: Did you know this about Capacity Planning in S/4HANA?
entry.content:
Hello Jenn,

Thanks for the interesting and thorough article - not much to find elsewhere on this subject 🙂

Can you help us clarify the following to questions:

1) While activating LiveCache in a RISE setup is assumed free of charge, are there any App Usage fees/licenses to be considered for apps utilizing the LiveCache?

2) Are there any further performance impacts to consider when activating LiveCache, like increase of CPU/RAM usage?

Thank you.

Enterprise Architecture - RISE RISE with SAP

entry.title: Re: Why ABAP do not have JSON de/serializer in kernel?
entry.content:
CALL TRANSFORMATION is also executed in the kernel (Core). And yes, you are right, it knows ABAP data, in some way, and can even create data (add new lines to known tables), but can not generate ABAP types (structures). And it is extremely fast, compared to implementations of IF_JSON_READER or IF_SXML_READER, because it does not have multiple callbacks from the kernel to ABAP runtime. At the same time, it is not flexible because of the simplification and normalization. ABAP is not JavaScript. ABAP is not case sensitive - you need to support different name conversion rules from JS to ABAP. It has a limit on the component name length, but JSON does not. ABAP does not have a built-in boolean (already has, but this does not help with old code)  - JSON has. ABAP has conversion exits. ABAP has much more complex data type support compared to JSON (different numerics, different types of tables, date+time+timestamp), and for all these, you need bidirectional conversion. It all costs performance. If you ask for customization of the conversion (and it will be asked), it will also affect performance. And at the end, you will not be much faster than a good implementation on top of IF_JSON_READER/IF_JSON_WRITER. 

But you may try with your initiative, but just be more precise and ask for something like CALL TRANSFORMATION id, but more flexible. 

I will also add my explanation and link to your CI request to my GitHub page to help people with similar requests 😉

P.S: I have already done a prototype of /ui2/cl_json with IF_JSON_READER/IF_JSON_WRITER - it may be ~40% faster, compared to ABAP only implementation, but some features, like type generation is quite difficult with this approach.

entry.title: Re: What is best way to integrate VS2022 with existing CR...
entry.content: Thanks Don. It appears my project will take a different turn. We use a 3rd party application which prompts users to enter in 4 parameters. Once done, those parameters are passed to a crystal report. The report has only one table, and my guess is that the application fetches the data from the right database and pushes it to the report. In our case, the schemas are the same across all databases. So I'm trying to learn the best way to push data from a DB to the report.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud: Custom Analytical Queries And Services; export no longer possible?
entry.content: We're experiencing the same issue.

entry.title: SAP Destination Service Troubleshooting
entry.content:
Destination Service Authentication Troubleshooting Made Simple: OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion Guide 

Troubleshoot like a pro with REST API validation! 

Configuration issues in OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion destinations can be tricky to diagnose. Destination Service: Troubleshooting Guide shows you a systematic approach using the Destination Service REST API to pinpoint errors quickly: 

We'll use a SuccessFactors integration example, but the same principles work for any target system using OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion or can be tested for other authentication types like SAMLAssertion. 

Understanding the Problem:  

When an integration fails, it’s important to pinpoint the root cause. Is the issue with the destination setup? User authentication? Token exchange? Rather than testing through your application which adds unnecessary complexity you can directly verify the destination by calling the Destination Service REST API.

This method separates the connectivity layer from your application logic. If you successfully obtain an access token, your destination is properly configured, meaning any remaining problems lie in your application code or the target system not in BTP connectivity.

What You'll Need Before Starting: 

Make sure you have Administrator access to your BTP subaccount, Space Manager or Developer permissions, a configured OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion destination, a REST client like Bruno or Postman, and verify that your test user exists in both your Identity Provider and the target system. 

For a complete step by step guide on how to achieve this check following KBA: 3708599 - Troubleshooting Guide for scenarios in BTP where Destination service is involved. 

Save this guide for faster troubleshooting, clearer error diagnosis, and confident destination configuration!

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation (IRPA) compatibility with Windows Server 2025
entry.content:
Hello,

We are currently evaluating an upgrade of our bot execution server to Windows Server 2025, and we would like to confirm the compatibility of SAP Build Process Automation (Desktop Agent / IRPA) with this operating system.

Our concern is whether running the bots on Windows Server 2025 could affect:

  1. SAP GUI scripting recognition
  2. Screen-based automation stability
  3. RDP session behavior
  4. Desktop Agent execution
  5. Session locking / auto logoff scenarios

Currently, the robots are running on Windows Server (previous version) without issues, but before upgrading we would like to confirm:

  1. Is Windows Server 2025 officially supported by SAP Build Process Automation Desktop Agent?
  2. Are there any known limitations or required configurations?
  3. Could there be impacts on SAP GUI automation or screen element recognition?

Any official documentation or SAP Notes references would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Multistage Intercompany Scenario - Logistics Integration
entry.content:
Hello @Mrinal_K_Roy 

Yes, freight cost allocation is also included. It's just not my topic, so I didn't mention it and restricted it to the logistics integration aspects.

Best regards,

Michael

entry.title: Type of store EWM without exit rules - pick point
entry.content:
Good afternoon, I share a concern that came to me, and I hope someone can help me with this: 
I request that for the type of warehouse AAAA, in warehouse AL01, the EWM configuration be changed so that it does not carry out withdrawal by FEFO. It must allow for the withdrawal of any batch. (This material is handled by HU and presents several batches).
This requirement arises because the product is stored in the type of warehouse AAAA in a chaotic way; when they are removed to the WIP (by means of the MF60), the operators remove the resin that they have most at hand. 
The idea is to ensure that the type of warehouse AAAA does not propose a batch when I create the warehouse task, but rather that when I perform picking in RF, I can **bleep** the batch that is closest.
It has been tested by removing the FEFO goods exit rules in SPRO for that type of warehouse: - Specify the search sequence for the type of warehouse
- Define warehouse exit control indicator
- Determine the warehouse type search sequence for output
Also, the output indicator from the master data in /scwm/mat1 was removed. With this it is achieved that the system does not let you create the task automatically but if by hand, but despite that it still proposes a particular batch.
It is impo

entry.title: Re: SF_LMS_Customize the Survey notification ItemEvaluati...
entry.content:
Hi @ChethanKumar_Ko 

Do you mean that your team wants the notification template to change dynamically based on the <SURVEY_ASSIGNMENT_TYPE>?

If so, that's not something that can be done... I mean, the notifications' tokens get populated dynamically based on the user / item / survey they are triggered for, but the notification's plain text stays the same.

You may consider reaching out to the SAP Professional Services team (https://me.sap.com/notes/2149831) to ask if this is something they can implement as a code customization.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Unable to submit quiz on SAP Learning
entry.content:
Hi  @iwon

For your reported scenario kindly check the resolution notes in the KBA 2404504 - How to get help with the SAP Learning Hub or SAP Training Center.

At the bottom of the SAP Training Centre or SAP Learning Hub page, click Visit the Help Center where you can find a ticketing system to request support with the Training/Learning Hub.

You can also contact the SAP Training Centre or SAP Learning Hub teams via the user support center available from the page.

BR

entry.title: Re: Unable to access practice systems in Learning Hub.

entry.title: People Analytics Story Report – How to extract LMS quiz questions and learner selected answers?
entry.content:
Hello !

I am building reports in People Analytics Stories and would like to extract data from SuccessFactors Learning (LMS).

 I need a Story Report that shows:

- the quiz question text

- and the answer selected by the learner

 I cannot find any dataset that contains the actual learner responses or selected answers.

My questions

1. Is it officially supported to retrieve learner question-level responses in Story Reports?

2. If yes, which dataset  should be used to access user responses ?

Any official confirmation, best practice, or documentation reference would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

SAP SuccessFactors Learning 

SAP SuccessFactors People Analytics

entry.title: PO and invoice got wiped out in business network test environment
entry.content:
What is probable cause of PO and Invoice being wiped out in the test environment for both Ariba Business Network and Supplier Portal. 

A sample process is defined below:

1. PO is created in Maximo - Successful 
2. PO flows to Ariba Business Network (BN) and then to Supplier portal (SP) 
      - the flow from BN to SP is not flowing and this caused the issue in test environment.
3. There is PO and Invoice data in Ariba test environment got wiped out - both in BN & SP

entry.title: Output Determination Management - Send out 2 emails with different templates
entry.content:
Hello,

We are implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602. Our customer sends out a Purchase Order document via email for every Purchase Order that is being created. That works. But there is another requirement: When the Purchasing Organization is PO10, there should be 2 emails send out to the Contact Person (CP). The first email should be the standard Purchase Order template, the second email should be a Shipping Label that is also sent by email. Our developer has already created the Form Template for the Shipping Label.

This is our current configuration in teh Output Parameter Determination app: 

 

So in short: When Purchasing Organization = PO10 --> Send 2 emails to the Contact Person (CP). Email 1: Purchase Order. Email 2: Shipping Label.

Can someone support with this question, is this possible in Cloud Publid Edition and how should we configure this? Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Time Off validation on start date
entry.content:
Hi @pkvarma ,

Use the ‘Get Absence In Days For Period Based On Working Days For Time Types()’ function within the take rule to trigger an error message. An approach similar to the example below should satisfy your requirement.

Regards,

VR

entry.title: Best Practice for Service PO Pricing Matrix (Thickness × Width) in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am designing a solution in SAP S/4HANA for an external processing service (e.g., industrial surface treatment) performed by a subcontractor.

We send Aluminium materials to the vendor, and they charge us based on a pricing matrix that depends on:

  • Thickness range (e.g., 0.40–0.49 mm, 0.50–0.59 mm, etc.)

  • Width range (e.g., 31–100 mm, 101–300 mm, etc.)

Each thickness/width combination has a fixed price. In total, there are around 80+ combinations.

We have a yearly contract with the vendor containing all agreed prices.

Current design proposal:

  • Create one generic service material (DIEN type).

  • Create a yearly quantity contract (MK).

  • Maintain one contract line per thickness/width combination, each with its specific price.

  • During PO creation, the buyer references the contract and manually selects the correct contract line.

Questions:

  1. Is this considered best practice in standard S/4HANA?

  2. Would you recommend creating multiple service materials instead (one per combination)?

  3. What are the practical benefits and drawbacks of using one generic service material versus creating 80 separate service material masters?

  4. Is there a standard way to automate price determination based on material characteristics (thickness, width) without custom development?

I would appreciate your feedback and practical experience on how you have handled similar pricing matrix scenarios.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Background Processing Framework
entry.content:
Hi @Molle ,

I ended up here through an asynchronous RAP request blog.

We will soon be upgrading to 2505, which will finally give us a few new features.

My question is, if I have understood correctly, do asynchronous RAP requests result in bgPF?

Asynchronous means that I send something and SAP takes care of it. Let's take an example of putting the API under load. What happens? Are all requests stored and processed one after the other depending on the load? Let's say the API normally handles 100 requests per minute without timeouts. What happens with 5,000 requests per minute?

entry.title: Wildcard incorrectly restricting data on a webi report
entry.content: I'm not sure if this is the right spot to post this but I am need of help on a webi report I've created. I have a universe that has a single table with 20 columns containing data on projects such as names, project codes, locations, etc.. On my webi report, I'm using 5 dimensions setup as "Matches Pattern" with the default value set as a wildcard. So for example one of the filters is on the mgr. Default value is set as the wildcard and regardless if there is a set manager or not within the data...it returns all data for the specific project. However, I have 1 filter for a project code that when the default value is left as the wildcard - NO data is returned for a project that has no value in the project code column. It ONLY returns data if a WBS Code is selected. Shouldn't the wildcard not restrict data when used? Otherwise, I am lost on why its restricting data.

Kind regards

Nic T.

entry.title: Comments for Multiple Candidates
entry.content:
Our recruiters are wondering if it's possible to add the same comment to multiple candidates at the same time. For example, there are times when they want to add a comment like "interviewed at job fair on Feb 24, 2026" to 10+ candidates. It would be a nice-to-have feature to allow them to put a checkmark next to those employees in the list and then add that same comment to all of them at the same time to save some time. They are beginning to use the new Reimagined Applicant view more often. 

Any ideas if something like this is possible? Thanks!

entry.title: Learning and Development Reporting
entry.content:
Is there reporting available for Learning and Development Module. 

We are trying to create dashboards to provide to employees to provide metrics on training completion and participation.  

Is there anything available? 

Our current thought is to back feed the data into a power bi report, but I would imagine there has to be something available in Success Factors natively.

We are currently undergoing a company restoration process, therefore, it is important to bulk update the status of the courses or learning activities created in the learning tool.

Reference image attached.

 

I look forward to your response and thank you in advance.

Sincerely,

entry.title: Re: What is best way to integrate VS2022 with existing CR...
entry.content:
Don is right, SAP does not support NuGet for Crystal. Install the latest Service Pack of SAP Crystal Reports from SAP and reference the assemblies from that install. Mixing CR 11 binaries with newer runtime libraries is what’s causing the dependency conflicts.

The SDK will load CR 11 reports just fine.

Given your scenario (same schema across databases), you have two solid options:

  • Dynamically set the DB connection at runtime via ReportDocument, or

  • Fetch the data yourself and push a DataTable into the report using SetDataSource().

If it is a single-table report, pushing the dataset is usually the cleaner and more stable approach.

entry.title: Careers Tab Permissions and External/Internal Candidate Conversion
entry.content: Hi, we have a legal business requirement to restrict access to our Internal Careers Tab for some of our employees. We updated the permissions accordingly, but didn't realize that this permission drives the proper conversion of External to Internal candidates. What we are seeing now is that when our external candidates are hired, a second internal candidate profile is being created instead of converting the external to internal. Now we have a significant number of employees who have both an internal and an external candidate profile. Has anyone had the same requirement and found a way to both restrict permissions to the Careers Tab and convert the external profile to an internal profile?

entry.title: How to change the extractor collection frequency to hourly in CSA for certificate stores
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We would like to know, how to change the extractor frequency in FRUN CSA for certain config stores like PSE certificate.
Currently our FRUN is at 5.0 version and we see the collection frequency defined as daily.
We want it to change to hourly across the FRUN system (only for certificate related stores) for the alerts to auto close.


Thanks and regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to change the extractor collection frequency to h...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

To change the data collection frequency of a specific extractor in CSA (Configuration & Security Analysis), follow the steps outlined in the KBA below:

  • 3721048 - How to Change Extractor Collection Frequency in CSA - SAP Focused Run - SAP for Me


P.S: Please note that after applying the changes, it may take up to one hour for them to be used by the system.


Thanks and best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Mismatch between ML Control Rec. and ML Period Totals...
entry.content: You may  have interrupted CKMLCP during processing,which caused an in consistency between the Material Ledger control record and the period totals data.You can try running CKMDUVMAT to repair the data and then attempt the posting again.

entry.title: abapgit-agent: AI Coding Assistants for ABAP
entry.content:
The Problem

AI coding assistants have transformed development, but ABAP developers are stuck manually copying code, activating objects, and feeding errors back to the AI.

The Solution

abapgit-agent bridges AI tools (Claude Code, Copilot, etc.) with your ABAP system:

AI generates code → Push to git → Agent pulls & activates → Returns errors → AI fixes
 

Features

  • pull - Pull from git and activate objects
  • inspect - Syntax check via Code Inspector
  • unit - Run AUnit tests
  • view / preview - Explore tables, classes, CDS views

Quick Start

npm install -g abapgit-agent
cd your-abap-repo
abapgit-agent init --folder /src/ --package ZMY_PACKAGE
# Edit .abapGitAgent with credentials
abapgit-agent pull

Prerequisites: abapGit (Developer version), Node.js 16+

Links
- GitHub: https://github.com/SylvosCai/abapgit-agent
- Docs: https://github.com/SylvosCai/abapgit-agent/blob/master/INSTALL.md

Feedback welcome!

entry.title: Re: abapgit-agent: AI Coding Assistants for ABAP
entry.content: Full documentation is available at: https://sylvoscai.github.io/abapgit-agent/

entry.title: SAP Support Potalのセキュリティパッチデーが更新されていない
entry.content:
ご担当各位
お世話になっております。


SAPサポートポータルのナレッジベースを見たところ、セキュリティパッチデーの欄において、パッチ発行予定日および発行実績がDIGITS年11月から更新されていないことに気づきました。

URL:
セキュリティパッチ発行予定は、更新されないのが正しい表記となるでしょうか？
上記の他に更新されているページ、或いは英語版で更新されたページがあれば、ご教授ください。

なお、本件について CIC に問い合わせたところ、SAP for Meにて問い合わせるよう回答をいただいておりました。

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Support Potalのセキュリティパッチデーが更新されていない
entry.content:
担当部署に確認したところ、現時点の方針では、日本語に翻訳されたHotNewsの情報は今後提供されず、

したがいまして、日本語翻訳された重要ノート (HotNews) 情報のページは更新されないとのことです。その旨ご了承ください。

新規・更新されたHotNewsノートにつきましては、SAP for Meのアプリケーション  https://me.sap.com/app/hotnews の利用をご検討ください。

(KBA 2342391 - SAP HotNews アプリケーションへのアクセスおよび使用方法 - SAP for Me - SAP for Me)

さらに、SAP for Meの通知機能をご利用いただくことも可能です。

通知設定については、2530034 - 電子メール、SMS、および/または SAP for Me 通知の管理方法 - SAP for Me - SAP for Me をご確認ください。

特定の月にリリースされたHotNewsをSAP HotNewsアプリケーションにて確認していただき、日本語に翻訳されていないものがありましたら、日本語翻訳をご依頼ください。

翻訳依頼方法の詳細につきましては、2595923 - SAP Notes および SAP KBA サービス情報: 日本語 - SAP for Meをご参照いただければと存じます。

entry.title: Missing Communications and notifications
entry.content:
I currently have subscriptions to get notifications for SAP Ariba Procurement, SAP Business Network, SAP Ariba Sourcing, SAP API Management, SAP Fieldglass, SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Runtime, and SAP - SAP Cloud Connector.

I would have expected that any degradation with relation to SAP Ariba Procurement but when * occurred I was not notified.  After consulting with other team members they did not receive emails as well.

Charter team needs to know how we can automatically be notified when items such as * occur.


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

Missing Emails

Unknown steps to reproduce.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Missing Communications and notifications
entry.content:
Because notifications like * are very special, it is documented in the KBA 3624413 - How to subscribe to Functional Degradation notifications for Ariba products - SAP for Me as following:

Email notifications for Functional Degradations are only sent to users who subscribe to the ongoing event via Get Support application. 

That is to say this type of notification can only be obtained through manual customer subscription. This is a standard design feature of the Ariba product, and its triggering logic is different from other events.

entry.title: Re: Technical AS-IS EA Canvas: Accelerating Assessments in Undocumented Environments
entry.content: Nice way to make the discovery more engaging and meaning full, helps even more in the scenario where there is no As-Is documentation in place. Bringing in right stakeholder is a critical success factor through. Thanks for sharing.

entry.title: Re: Bienvenidos al grupo de SAP Community Mexico
entry.content:
Hola, soy Abril 👋 👸

Llevo casi 20 años en el mundo del reclutamiento y prácticamente toda mi vida profesional he estado ligada a SAP. Me ha tocado ver consultores crecer desde perfiles junior hasta convertirse en arquitectos, líderes de proyecto o directores… y eso es algo que disfruto muchísimo.

Me apasiona conectar a personas con oportunidades que realmente hagan sentido para su momento de vida, no solo profesionalmente, sino también personal. Siempre he creído que un buen match no es solo técnico, también es humano y hasta energético ✨

¿En qué parte de México estás basada, o a dónde viajas?

Soy de Monterrey, NL, pero desde hace más de 10 años vivo en San Miguel de Allende, Gto. Eso me ha permitido equilibrar el ritmo corporativo con una vida más consciente.

Trabajo de manera remota 👩‍💻y viajo solo cuando algún proyecto lo requiere o por placer 🏖.

¿En qué área estás trabajando hoy?

Principalmente en reclutamiento especializado SAP (perfiles senior, arquitectos y posiciones estratégicas).

Aunque en los últimos años también he estado reclutando en otras industrias, y eso me ha hecho aprender muchísimo sobre distintos modelos de negocio, culturas organizacionales y estilos de liderazgo. Todos los días aprendo algo nuevo, y eso me encanta. 💫

¿Qué estás aprendiendo o qué te da curiosidad ahora?

Estoy aprendiendo a usar mejor SAP Learning Hub, explorando la herramienta para entender cómo puede ayudar a los consultores a certificarse, actualizarse y seguir creciendo en el ecosistema SAP. Me interesa mucho acompañarlos no solo a encontrar oportunidades, sino a prepararse estratégicamente para ellas.

¿Un interés fuera de SAP?

El yoga y la meditación son parte esencial de mi vida 🧘🏻‍♀️
Me ayudan a mantener claridad, equilibrio y enfoque en un entorno que suele ser muy acelerado.

También me apasiona todo lo relacionado con crecimiento personal y conciencia.

Estoy aquí para conectar, aprender, compartir y seguir construyendo relaciones reales, no solo contactos. 🤍

Gracias por este espacio @MariettaLopez , Edgar y Sandra! 👏

entry.title: RBP Troubleshooting Tool Issue
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I'm using the RBP Troubleshooting tool to compare permissions between 2 Users and it's showing them as matching. Upon 'Showing All' and clicking on one of the Roles it takes me to the Permission Role as expected. However the Permission Groups Granted and Target that it is showing in that Permission Role is wrong. Neither of the Users are in either the Granted nor Target Groups.
They are in a different Permission Group that is assigned to that same Permission Role, but not the one it was showing me. It seems like a fatal-flaw with the RBP Troubleshooting tool if it's meant to be narrowing down the RBP issues, but showing you the wrong Matched Assignment Groups.

Has anyone else come across this? 

Is this a SAP error that needs fixing?

Many thanks for your advice!

entry.title: Sorry, it is not possible to maintain payment information if there is no job information or .....
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Good day!

We are now migrating our ONB to 2.0 and find the below issue while also adding payment info as requirement.

May I kindly ask if there's already a concrete resolution for this issue?

I've noticed that there's a patch but why is the error still existent? 

I also checked this KBAs, however, no resolution indicated.

  • 3219782 - Payment Information validation error in MPH or NHDR - Onboarding
  • 3673875 - Error when changing payment information start date in add new employee - Employee Central
  • 3194252 - [Onboarding] Sorry, it is not possible to maintain payment information if there is no job information or no country/region in job information.

Thank you very much in advance for the help and inputs.

 

Best Regards,

Gelo

entry.title: Re: SAP Certified - SAP Generative AI Developer (C_AIG_New Format)
entry.content:
Hi Filizoeztuerk,

Would you mind sharing what learning you did and what you practiced hands on?

I am not sure what type of practical questions we get, would it be the AI Launchpad or do we need to know sdk as well?

Please suggest your experience. Thanks!

entry.title: Need to be able to download SAP Ariba Compliance Documents
entry.content:
We need to download SAP Ariba compliance documents such as SOC 1 from our SAP for Me account. Could you assist with this request?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Need to be able to download SAP Ariba Compliance Docu...
entry.content:
To access the 'Find Compliance Documents' card, the 'Access Compliance Documents authorization' must be assigned to your S-user ID. 

For security purposes, this authorization can only be granted by your company's Super Administrator(s). You can find their contact information on the  Important Contacts page.

Additionally, you may find the below KBA helpful, as it provides detailed information about the S-user ID authorizations required for the 'Find Compliance Documents' card. 

3629697 - S-user ID authorizations required for Compliance Documents Finder applications - SAP for Me

Best Regards,  
Judy  
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA Custom Measure
entry.content:
Hi Alex,

It is recommended to create a new custom "Days to Offer" measure instead of updating the calculation of the existing "Days to Fill" and "Days to Hire" measures as they might be used for deriving other Result Measure (eg. Time to Fill per Hire etc.) .

You can either engage your CSP/ CEE or a qualified partner to implement the new custom measure.

Thank you.

Kind Regards,

Wei Cher Chen

entry.title: Library Results Booster - Tiers & Weight
entry.content:
Hello

I'm keen to hear from those who have used the Learning Library's search results booster features: Search Tiers and/or Search Weight. 

What worked/didn't work for you? How did you set it up? Did you use something else and did that work better/worse? 

Any and all responses appreciated. 

Thank you in advance.

Sarah

entry.title: Re: No System Alias Assignment and GSDO Group Maintained(...
entry.content: you can change the routing to co-deployed and activate the ICF node.

entry.title: SAP for ME Greyed Out Contact Field For New Subscription
entry.content:
SAP for ME Greyed Out Contact Field For New Subscription

Also, can you share a documentation on how the SAP for Me roles work? What do they enable or control? Thank you.


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

Steps:
1. SAP for ME
2. Systems & Provisioning
3. Get Notified
4. Add New Subscription
5. Contact field is greyed out

Also, can you share a documentation on how the SAP for Me roles work? What do they enable or control? Thank you.




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP for ME Greyed Out Contact Field For New Subscription
entry.content:
The contact field greyed out is currently unavailable because you are viewing the My Subscriptions section. This particular view is designed to allow you to manage or add new subscriptions exclusively for your own S-user. 
 
If you need to manage subscriptions for other S-users, please switch to the Manage Subscriptions view. For further guidance, you can refer to the KBA 2896942 - How Super and Cloud Administrators manage Cloud Notification subscriptions for other users - SAP for Me

For detailed steps on how individual S-users can manage their own Cloud Notification subscriptions, refer to KBA 2900069 - How S-users manage their own Cloud Notification subscriptions - SAP for Me
If you have further questions or need additional assistance, please don’t hesitate to reach out.  
Best Regards,  
Judy  
SAP Support  

Best Regards,  
Judy  
SAP Support

entry.title: Resolving Key Mapping Missing Issues while sending Freight Unit from Cloud ERP to On Premise system
entry.content:
Introduction

The process for Scope Item 6W1 for External Transportation Planning enables transportation requests created in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP to be planned within an SAP On Premise system. In this case, Freight units generated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP can be transferred to the On Premise system (with SAP TM) for detailed transportation planning.

Once planning is completed, the results are sent back and integrated into SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP in the form of freight orders. Upon transfer, transportation charges for these freight orders are calculated automatically. Based on the finalized freight orders, outbound deliveries can then be initiated.

This post covers the scenario where an error is received and the possible workaround for the error.

Kindly note that below steps are a workaround and steps must be taken to resolve error by undertaking the master data replication as per the set up guide.

Process Details

Complete the set up for the scenario including the set up of communication scenario and Data replication. The process requires the Purchase order be created within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP. Ensure that the Purchase group is selected as "006" External TM Planning (refer below screenshot).

Based on settings a Transportation Management Freight unit is created. The same can be seen within the Purchase order app under the tab "TM Status"

As the Process needs the Freight Unit planning within the external TM system the scope items 6W1 in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP should be activated. Once done the Freight Unit from Public Cloud system is replicated to the On Premise system.

This replication message should create a Freight Unit within the On Premise / Decentralized TM system.

Challenge

In case of Failure of the FU replication, check the message within the SAP Message Monitor to identify the type of error. One of the error that can occur may relate to Invalid data. As you can see in the below screenshot the error related to invalid data.

This blog covers on the process to be followed to resolve this issue.

To resolve the issue, as you can see the below warnings need to be removed to resolve the error. These warning relate to incorrect / missing key mapping for different Master Data Objects like Location / Business Partner / Product between the Public Cloud and the On Premise system. This can happen in case the replication was not performed correctly.

Resolution

To resolve the issue, key mapping needs to be maintained in both the Public Cloud and the On Premise system.

You can follow the following steps for the same.

  1. Use the Tcode MDG_KM_MAINTAIN within the On Premise SAP system. Manage Key Mapping UI opens.

  1. For each of the impacted Business Object Type (as per the error) select the Object Type (example Business Partner in above screenshot) and Object Type ID, Business System and the Master Data ID (example Business partner ID).
  2. Click on Add Row to add the entries as below. Fill in the details such that there are 2 rows each of Public cloud and On Premise system
    1. Row 1 with Public Cloud System ID and BP number and Row 2 for BP UUID in the standard format: 8-4-4-4-12 (hexadecimal groupings). Example – If location UUID is 355F4B6A67ED1EEE9297A3E7521BF064 it should be mentioned as 355F4B6A-67ED-1EEE-9297-A3E7521BF064
    2. Repeat the same for the On Premise system with corresponding details added in row 3 & 4.

 

  1. Save
  2. Repeat the same process from steps 1 to 4 for all other Master data objects as per the error.
  3. Repeat the same process from steps 1 to 5 within the Public Cloud system using the app „Manage Key Mapping“.

Rerun the process of FU replication and mapping error should be resolved now.
